mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal a strengthening labor market, which drives higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations. This leads to positive equity returns as the economic recovery gains traction.
failure_condition: A positive 3-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days in more than 50% of instances during a 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) breaches a high absolute threshold, it confirms severe economic distress, triggering investor risk aversion and expectations of widespread earnings declines, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: In the 20 trading days following UNRATE crossing above 9.5%, the S&P 500 shows positive returns in more than 60% of triggered events.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate policy (ZIRP) environment, an acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) is amplified into equity prices. Low financing costs mean production growth translates more efficiently into profit growth, signaling a robust recovery.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate at zero lower bound since Dec 2008
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the correlation between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During a low-inflation regime, strong growth in retail sales (RSAFS) signals healthy consumer demand without stoking fears of monetary tightening. This is unambiguously positive for corporate revenues and equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Low inflation and disinflationary pressures post-GFC
failure_condition: In periods where YoY CPIAUCSL is below 2%, the rank correlation between RSAFS 3-month growth and 20-day forward returns is less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) from depressed levels indicates a bottoming of the housing market and renewed construction activity. This has strong positive multiplier effects on the broader economy, boosting investor sentiment and equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market collapse was central to the GFC
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the 6-month change in HOUST predicting 60-day forward returns fails to remain positive over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, unexpected drop in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in the economy's supply side, leading to downward revisions of GDP and earnings forecasts, causing negative equity returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production fell sharply during the GFC
failure_condition: Instances where INDPRO monthly growth is below -1.0% are not followed by statistically significant negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed Funds rate is near the zero lower bound, an upside surprise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) reduces real rates and could signal stronger pricing power. However, it primarily raises fears of a premature withdrawal of accommodation, increasing discount rates and hurting equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Market sensitivity to inflation data is heightened at ZIRP
failure_condition: During ZIRP, months with CPIAUCSL MoM change > 0.3% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: An interaction where real GDP growth (GDPC1) is positive but retail sales (RSAFS) are decelerating points to a weakening consumer, who is the primary driver of the economy. This divergence signals a future slowdown and leads to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ change > 0 AND RSAFS 3-month change < 0
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is ~70% of US GDP
failure_condition: The S&P 500 provides positive 60-day forward returns in over 50% of quarters where the trigger condition (positive GDP growth, negative retail sales growth) is met.
